_comment: MANDATORY: after editing this file, run `python ops/apply_railway_service_config.py --all` BEFORE the next push. Service-instance fields (deploy.<service>.{startCommand, cronSchedule, restartPolicyType, source.repo, watchPatterns}) live on Railway's service-instance config and are only updated via the GraphQL serviceInstanceUpdate mutation. Without the apply, push triggers a rebuild but the new container still runs with the OLD startCommand/cron/etc — you get a build-vs-runtime config drift that's painful to diagnose (build log shows the new buildCommand; runtime crashes anyway because startCommand wasn't propagated). The only field Railway actually reads from this file at build time is `build.buildCommand`. See docs/OPERATIONS.md §1 'Build layout' and the project memory entry feedback_apply_after_railway_json.md.
_architecture: THREE Railway services (consolidated 2026-05-15, was 5): platform-pipeline (daily data + engine sweep), trade-monitor (24/7 fill listener), allocator (weekly capital rebalance). The local launchd daemons (data_operations cron + engine-service poller + allocator + trade-monitor) remain the canonical Mac path; this file is the Railway deployment shape. The prior shape (sigma-scheduler, reversion-scheduler, vector-scheduler, ingestion-engine, trade-monitor) decomposed each engine into its own service and used a long-running ingestion-engine for data refresh — replaced by ops.py --update which orchestrates 15 stages in one process. Hobby tier caps at 5 services; we use 3 to leave headroom for a dashboard or health endpoint.
_csv_archive_env_vars: R3 archive-substrate migration (PR feat/csv-archive-pluggable-backend-r3, 2026-05-21). Set these in Railway's service-variables UI (NOT here — secrets, and Railway reads service vars from its own store, not from railway.json). Unset → ephemeral local FS, which is the broken-on-Railway state R3 fixes. Required values when migrating: CSV_ARCHIVE_BACKEND=s3, CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_ENDPOINT=<host:port>, CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_BUCKET=<bucket>, CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_KEY_ID=<access-key>, CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_SECRET=<secret-key>. Optional: CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_REGION (non-AWS S3-compatibles can leave blank), CSV_ARCHIVE_S3_SECURE='false' only for an in-cluster MinIO over plain HTTP (default 'true'). The exact bucket-provider choice (Railway native object store vs R2 vs Supabase Storage) is an infra decision pending Railway re-enable per project_railway_hobby_tier — the seam is generic across all S3-compatibles.

build._build_note: Build into an explicit venv at /app/.deps (NOT /app/.venv — .gitignore line 8 strips that path from the runtime copy). Packages live inside /app, which Railpack copies wholesale into the runtime image; the venv's python binary symlinks back to the mise-managed interpreter at /mise/installs/python/<ver>/bin/python, which IS also copied. Why this over installing into mise's site-packages directly: Railpack only preserves the /mise/installs symlink, not its real target under /root/.local/share/mise/... so `/mise/shims/python -m pip install` succeeds at build time but the packages vanish on stage handoff. The venv-in-/app pattern sidesteps that entirely.

platform-pipeline._comment: Daily data refresh + engine sweep, sequential in one process. Replaces the four-cron decomposition (sigma/reversion/vector schedulers + ingestion-engine) of the prior shape. Cron: 21:30 UTC weekdays — 90 min after NYSE close (winter EST) and 30 min after (summer EDT), so daily bars are settled. ops.py --update refuses during regular session if invoked manually; the cron timing avoids that by construction. Forensics is the final stage of --update (added 2026-05-15), so dossiers are refreshed in-line. Engine sweep runs after data refresh succeeds; failure of --update aborts the run before engines fire (stale data would produce bad signals).

trade-monitor._comment: Persistent service — consumes Alpaca trade_updates and writes platform.open_orders + platform.aar_events. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-12-trade-monitor-design.md. Requires ipv6EgressEnabled=true (Supabase direct connection) and ALPACA_KEY/ALPACA_SECRET/ALPACA_PAPER in service variables. NOTE: must run as `python -m tpcore.trade_monitor` (not `python tpcore/trade_monitor.py`) — invoking the file directly puts tpcore/ on sys.path, which makes stdlib `import logging` resolve to the project's tpcore.logging package and crash with a circular-import error during interpreter bootstrap.

allocator._comment: Weekly cross-engine capital rebalance via inverse-vol weighting + CHOP gating + drift thresholds. Reads AARs through tpcore.aar.AARReader (shared with forensics). Cron: Monday 13:00 UTC (= 09:00 ET, pre-open) so any rebalance instructions are live before sigma/reversion/vector schedulers fire that evening. Locally invoked via ops.py --allocate; on Railway via the start command directly.
